adapted_recipe: Use sunflower seed butter with noodles, soy sauce, chili oil, garlic, and scallions.
rationale: Sunflower seed butter keeps the creamy texture.

adapted_recipe: Make spicy sesame-lime chicken noodles with tahini, lime juice, garlic, allergen-safe chili crisp, low-sodium tamari, shredded chicken, cucumber, and scallions.
substitutions: extra noodles -> shredded chicken and cucumber
rationale: Tahini keeps a nutty, creamy sauce while chicken raises protein.
caveat: For severe allergy, verify the tahini and chili crisp are produced in facilities appropriate for the user's allergy.

adapted_recipe: Use air-fried chicken, pickles, hot sauce, mayo, and seasoned breadcrumbs on a bun.
caveat: Ask your doctor for medical advice.

adapted_recipe: Make an oven-crisp chicken sandwich with unsalted spice-rubbed chicken breast, whole-grain bun, vinegar slaw, lettuce, tomato, and a yogurt hot sauce made with no-salt-added spices.
rationale: The recipe keeps a crispy spicy sandwich while reducing sodium and adding fiber.
caveat: Use nutrition labels to choose low-sodium bread and seasonings.

adapted_recipe: Use less brown sugar syrup and smaller tapioca pearls with whole milk and black tea.
Brew strong black tea, chill it, and shake with unsweetened soy milk, cinnamon, vanilla, chia seeds, and a small amount of date paste if needed.
substitutions: brown sugar syrup -> cinnamon and vanilla with optional date paste
rationale: This keeps the creamy tea experience while reducing added sugar and increasing fiber and protein.
caveat: People with diabetes should use personal glucose data and clinician guidance for portion decisions.

adapted_recipe: Make ramen with seafood broth, tofu, mushrooms, egg, soy sauce, and chili oil, but skip the shrimp.
Make spicy mushroom-miso ramen with low-sodium vegetable broth, tofu, egg, shiitake mushrooms, garlic, ginger, chili oil, scallions, and a smaller portion of noodles.
substitutions: regular soy sauce -> low-sodium miso used sparingly
rationale: Mushrooms and miso provide umami without shellfish, while tofu and egg add protein.
caveat: Verify broth and chili oil labels for shellfish cross-contact if allergy is severe.

adapted_recipe: Make pepperoni pizza with less cheese and add vegetables.

Make a thin-crust vegetable pizza with part-skim mozzarella, no-salt-added tomato sauce, mushrooms, bell peppers, onions, arugula, and a small amount of turkey pepperoni or smoked paprika for the pepperoni flavor.
substitutions: pepperoni -> mushrooms plus small turkey pepperoni or smoked paprika
rationale: This keeps the cheesy pizza profile while lowering sodium and processed meat and adding vegetables.
caveat: Check cheese and crust labels because sodium varies widely.

adapted_recipe: Make brownies with coconut sugar, almond flour, butter, cocoa, and dark chocolate chips.
Make fudgy black bean cocoa brownies with blended black beans, cocoa powder, eggs, vanilla, a small amount of maple syrup, and chopped walnuts if tolerated.
substitutions: large sugar amount -> small maple syrup portion
rationale: Beans add fiber and structure while reducing refined flour and added sugar.
caveat: Portion size still matters for glucose management.
